Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Given the log, we see multiple documents and tasks: Document1.docx (drafting), Email about Annual Meeting, Report_Draft.pdf, Budget_2024.xlsx, and Quarterly_Report.docx.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user starts with focus on Quarterly_Report.docx, then switches to Document1.docx, works on it, then switches to email, then to PDF, then to Excel, then back to Document1.docx, closes it, then works on Quarterly_Report.docx again. So there are multiple threads. Cases could be per document or per task.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For case identification, we can assign a unique case ID per task or document. For example: Case: Quarterly_Report.docx (start and end) Case: Document1.docx (interleaved) Case: Email about Annual Meeting Case: Report_Draft.pdf Case: Budget_2024.xlsx

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But theres a complication: The user works on Document1.docx, then switches to email, then to PDF, then to Excel, then back to Document1.docx... Better approach: Use the window title as the case identifier.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For SWITCH events, we need to decide. Since SWITCH indicates a change of focus, we could assign it to the case that is being left or entered... Alternatively, we can omit SWITCH events and only keep the focus, typing, etc... We can treat FOCUS as the event that starts or continues a case, and SWITCH as a transition.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now, we need to define activity names. Raw actions: FOCUS, TYPING, SAVE... For example: FOCUS on a document: Open Document or Switch to Document... TYPING: Type Text or Edit Document. SAVE: Save Document... Use a verb-noun format: Edit Document, Save Document, Close Document, Open Document, Read Email, Send Email, Annotate PDF

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets go through each raw event: 1. 2024-12-11T08:59:50.000Z, FOCUS... => Case 1, Activity: Open Document 2. 2024-12-11T09:00:00.000Z, FOCUS... => Case 2, Activity: Open Document 3. TYPING... => Case 2, Activity: Edit Document

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: After the SWITCH from Word to Chrome, there is no FOCUS event on Chrome; the next event is CLICK. Similarly for others. At 09:05:00 FOCUS on Excel directly after HIGHLIGHT on PDF. So we have both FOCUS and SWITCH. The FOCUS events act as switch to indicators.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Given the complexity, Ill adopt a pragmatic approach: For any event that sets focus to a particular window (FOCUS or SWITCH), we create an event Activate Document for the target case.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus, we create events for all raw events mapped accordingly. Now we have events for each case. We need to produce a table with columns: Case ID, Activity Name, Timestamp
